subject: Geography
title: Fault-Block Mountains
description: A quick win: understand Fault-Block Mountains.

hook.text: Sometimes the Earth's crust doesn't fold—it cracks. Huge blocks tilt skyward.

buildup.text: Fault-block mountains form when tension pulls the crust apart along fracture lines.

discovery.text: The Sierra Nevada in California is a massive tilted block, steep on one side, gentle on the other.

twist.text: East Africa's Rift Valley is pulling apart right now, creating new fault-block ranges.

climax.text: In millions of years, the rift may split Africa in two, creating a new ocean.

punchline.text: Some mountains rise because the ground cracks apart.

quiz.question: How do fault-block mountains form?
options: Earth's crust cracks and blocks tilt upward
